rationales.tradition: Lighting incense because 'my grandmother would approve' shows him leaning on an inherited family ritual before the client call.

rationales.self_direction: He insists that he can 'run my own thing' instead of taking over the family business, affirming his own chosen path.
rationales.conformity: Rather than argue, he 'changed the subject to Jason's wedding plans,' deliberately smoothing over the social tension.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on 'Sunday dinner at Ah Ma's,' her decades-old recipe, and a family migration story that ties his present back to earlier generations.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the evening with family and is moved that Ah Ma checks whether he is 'eating enough and sleeping enough,' showing closeness to his people.

rationales.self_direction: He chose to 'start a tech consultancy' instead of staying in the family company and is still defending that decision as his own.
rationales.tradition: Looking up the village in Fujian and tracing 'three generations of building' shows him actively locating himself within his family lineage rather than discarding it.

rationales.achievement: 'Closed the deal with the logistics company' and landing a paid three-month engagement is a clear success marker in his work.
rationales.tradition: When his mother asks about Qing Ming, he answers 'yes, I'll be there' without hesitation, treating the family observance as a given priority.

rationales.conformity: When his uncle insults his work at the grave, he 'just smiled' because he 'didn't want to cause a scene,' restraining himself to preserve harmony.
rationales.tradition: He joins Qing Ming fully by carrying offerings, burning paper money, and doing 'the three bows' at his grandfather's grave.

rationales.self_direction: He tells his father 'I just want to build my own thing,' clearly defending an independent path.
rationales.conformity: The night turns into a 'real' fight with his father, showing open resistance rather than going along with family expectations.

rationales.tradition: He goes to the temple on the lunar schedule, learns to hold the joss sticks properly, and embraces 'being part of something older than yourself.'
rationales.benevolence: He joins the visit partly because 'she seemed pleased when I offered,' showing care for Ah Ma through shared time and attention.

rationales.security: He turns down 'probably six months of runway,' knowingly giving up financial cushion for principle.
rationales.tradition: He refuses the gambling client because using his grandfather's name that way felt 'wrong' and conflicted with the family story about gambling ruining an earlier generation.
The birthday dinner follows a long-running family pattern at 'the one we've been going to since I was a kid,' and the jade bracelet fits that inherited style.
rationales.benevolence: He shows up for his mother's birthday and gives her 'a jade bracelet,' a concrete act of care despite the tension with his father.

rationales.achievement: He works until '9pm' to stabilize the client's site during a promotion, prioritizing competent performance under pressure.
rationales.tradition: Missing 'Sunday dinner at Ah Ma's' for the first time in years breaks a recurring family practice he had reliably kept.
rationales.benevolence: He explicitly frames the day as choosing 'the client over the grandmother,' putting work ahead of a close family relationship.

rationales.tradition: The visit includes Ah Ma's braised pork, her paper offerings for Vesak Day, and a story linking him back to his grandfather's habits.
rationales.benevolence: He goes to pick up the food she saved for him and spends time sitting with her, showing renewed attention to a close family bond.

rationales.tradition: She lights 'a candle at my altar,' says a prayer, and explicitly says 'that's what we do in my family' when explaining how she responds to grief.
rationales.benevolence: After losing the child patient, she stays 'an extra hour' holding his mother's hand and says she would 'do it again tomorrow.'

rationales.security: She likes the apartment idea because it would 'cut costs' and living with family feels trustworthy and dependable.
rationales.benevolence: She wants Marisol nearby because 'she's family, so I trust her,' emphasizing closeness with a specific loved one.

rationales.tradition: She recreates Posadas with songs, luminarias, tamales, and reflections about the 'thread connecting me to where I come from.'
rationales.benevolence: She organizes the celebration for pediatric patients and brings in food so the children can share in it, extending warmth and care to people around her.

rationales.self_direction: She admits she is 'curious' about the offer but shuts that impulse down as 'selfish,' subordinating her own exploratory desire.
rationales.stimulation: She rejects travel nursing because it would mean 'no knowing where I'll be month to month' and says 'I'm supposed to be building something stable, not chasing adventures.'
rationales.security: Her reasons center on a 'consistent schedule,' saving for the condo, and staying where her support system already is.
rationales.conformity: Calling the alternative 'selfish' and saying 'I'm supposed to be' doing something else shows her aligning herself with expected obligations.
rationales.benevolence: She stays partly because 'my cousin is counting on me' and because her family 'gave up a lot' for her, prioritizing those close ties.

rationales.security: Her New Year list focuses on 'get the condo' and 'building something solid, something that will last,' showing a strong pull toward stability.
rationales.tradition: Nochebuena, midnight mass, incense, and changing the water for her santos all show deliberate continuity with inherited religious and family practices.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the holiday with cousins and includes 'help Marisol settle in' and 'call home more often' in her plans for the coming year.

rationales.security: Setting up 'automatic transfers to my savings account' so the balance grows each month is a direct move toward financial stability.

rationales.conformity: She snaps back at Rachel and says 'I'm not going to change who I am to make Rachel comfortable,' openly rejecting local pressure to adapt.
rationales.tradition: She defends calling Mami and says 'we've been this way for generations,' grounding her response in inherited family patterns.
rationales.benevolence: The whole conflict comes from how strongly she values staying connected to her mother and treating family closeness as non-negotiable.

rationales.security: She is distressed because she had 'planned around this' condo and now has to 'start over,' showing how much she wanted a stable housing plan.
rationales.benevolence: The condo mattered because it meant being near 'our other cousins' and close enough for easy 'Sunday dinners,' centering close family life.

rationales.tradition: She decides that whatever she builds 'will have roots' and says 'keeping our ways alive' can continue even across distance, reaffirming continuity with those before her.
rationales.benevolence: The breakthrough comes through a deep conversation with Abuela and renewed attachment to 'her, and Mami, and everyone who came before,' emphasizing close family bonds.
